structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV for Standard 62.1 - SZVAV AHUs
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: For compliance with the Ventilation Rate Procedure of ASHRAE Standard 62.1-2016
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp equals Voz. The CO2 control loop output resets Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone has CO2 sensor and is in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set MinOAsp = Voz. Reset Vbz-P* from 0 (0% output) to Vbz-P (100% output) using P-only CO2 loop.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz-P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz-P at 100% loop output.

evidence_quote: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz-P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz-P at 100% loop output.
evidence_text: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp is equal to Voz. The CO2 control loop output shall reset the population component of the required breathing zone outdoor airflow Vbz-P* from 0 L/s (0 cfm) at 0% loop output up to Vbz-P at 100% loop output.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 DCV for Title 24 - Cooling-Only, Reheat, Series Fan-Powered, Dual-Duct Terminal Units
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Cooling-only VAV, reheat VAV, constant-volume series fan-powered, dual-duct VAV terminal units with mixing control and inlet airflow sensors, dual-duct VAV with mixing control and discharge airflow sensor, or dual-duct VAV with cold-duct minimum control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: During Occupied Mode, a P-only CO2 loop resets Vmin* from Vmin (0% output) to Vcool-max (50% output). The loop output from 50% to 100% is used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone has CO2 sensor and is in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Enable P-only CO2 loop. Reset Vmin* from Vmin (0% output) to Vcool-max (50% output). Loop output 50-100% used at AHU level.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The CO2 control loop output shall reset the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool-max at 50%, as shown in Figure 5.2.1.4-1. The loop output from 50% to 100% will be used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum; see AHU controls.

evidence_quote: The CO2 control loop output shall reset the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool-max at 50%, as shown in Figure 5.2.1.4-1. The loop output from 50% to 100% will be used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum; see AHU controls.
evidence_text: The CO2 control loop output shall reset the occupied minimum airflow setpoint Vmin* from the zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin at 0% up to maximum cooling airflow setpoint Vcool-max at 50%, as shown in Figure 5.2.1.4-1. The loop output from 50% to 100% will be used at the system level to reset outdoor air minimum; see AHU controls.

structured_payload_candidate.summary: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp is reset based on the zone CO2 control-loop signal from MinOA (0% signal) to DesOA (100% signal).

structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset MinOAsp from MinOA (0% CO2 loop signal) to DesOA (100% CO2 loop signal).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The minimum outdoor air setpoint MinOAsp shall be reset based on the zone CO2 control-loop signal from MinOA at 0% signal to DesOA at 100% signal.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) Pulse Width Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than the lowest possible setpoint Vm, the airflow setpoint is pulse width modulated with a total cycle time of 15 minutes (adjustable). Open period OP is the larger of 1.5 minutes or TCT * TAVratio. Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During closed period, damper control loop disabled and output set to 0. At end of closed period, damper commanded to last position from previous open period before reenabling loop.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Vspt is nonzero and less than Vm
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Calculate TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. Set TCT = 15 minutes (adjustable). Open period OP = max(1.5 minutes, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During open period, Vspt* = Vm. During closed period, Vspt* = 0, disable VAV damper control loop with output 0. At end of closed period, command damper to last position from previous open period, then reenable loop. Repeat until Vspt > Vm.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows: a. The time-averaged ventilation (TAV) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable) c. Open period. During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint Vspt* shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of the following: d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio f. Closed period. During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper control loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling the control loop. g. During TAV mode, each cycle shall consist of an open and closed period that alternate until Vspt is greater than Vm.

evidence_quote: 5.2.2.1.  When the active airflow setpoint  Vspt is nonzero  and is less than the lowest possible airflow

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAV Mode Initial Open Period with Randomization
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM * OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Start with initial open period = RNDM * OP.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0.

evidence_quote: When first entering TAV mode, start with an initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is a random number between 0.0 and 1.0.

structured_payload_candidate.title: CO2 Sensor Out of Calibration Alarm (TAV)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and CO2 exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm indicating sensor may be out of calibration.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: CO2 < 300 ppm OR (Unoccupied Mode > 2 hours AND CO2 > 600 ppm)
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Generate Level 3 alarm with text identifying sensor and indicating it may be out of calibration.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: CO2 concentration < 300 ppm OR (Unoccupied Mode > 2 hours AND CO2 > 600 ppm)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration.

evidence_quote: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration.
evidence_text: If the CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone-Des-OA-min Calculation for Title 24
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Zone-Des-OA-min is set to zero if window open or if occupancy sensor and unpopulated with occupied-standby permitted; otherwise set to the larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Priority: 1) Zero if window open. 2) Zero if occupancy sensor, unpopulated, and occupied-standby permitted. 3) Otherwise, larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Zone-Des-OA-min is equal to the following, in order from highest to lowest priority: i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open. ii. Zero if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and is permitted to be in occupied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3. iii. The larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min otherwise.

evidence_quote: Zone-Des-OA-min is equal to the following, in order from highest to lowest priority: i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open. ii. Zero if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and is permitted to be in occupied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3. iii. The larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min otherwise.
evidence_text: Zone-Des-OA-min is equal to the following, in order from highest to lowest priority: i. Zero if the zone has a window switch and the window is open. ii. Zero if the zone has an occupancy sensor, is unpopulated, and is permitted to be in occupied-standby mode per Section 3.1.1.2.b.3. iii. The larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min otherwise.
